items.title: Liberal frontbencher Jonno Duniam to retire

children.content: Senator Duniam says it is time to prioritise his family after 25 years in politics, and he will leave his position in the Senate by the end of this year.

gallery.altText: Johnno Duniam addresses press conference with Pter Dutton and Manny Cicchiello. Electorate: Aston

thumbnail.altText: Johnno Duniam addresses press conference with Pter Dutton and Manny Cicchiello. Electorate: Aston

items.title: A cafe run by students, for students aims to tackle food insecurity

children.content: It might look like a regular cafe, but a new student-run business in Hobart's CBD has a different goal than turning a profit — a mission to feed their struggling peers.

image.altText: A woman smiles for a photo in a mini grocery store

items.title: As work modernises in Australia, some rare trades face decline

children.content: A shortage of locksmiths in Australia persists despite government-funded incentives. The nation's changing labour landscape has meant some specialist trades are not being chosen by apprentices.

image.altText: a man in a navy shirt outside a white trade van on a street

items.title: Hip-thrusting, flannel-wearing drag kings say Hobart's embraced them

children.content: Hobart's drag kings say the closure of a gay bar pushed their performances into heteronormative spaces and a surprisingly accepting Tasmanian community.

image.altText: A drag king winking in front of a crowd

items.title: New line of hope in discovering the Nannup Four

children.content: The vice-president of a search team that solved two high-profile missing persons cases in Tasmania turns his attention to the Nannup Four case after it was featured in the Expanse podcast.

image.altText: A photo of two men, a woman and a girl with a photo of bushland in the background.

items.title: Greens say premier's answers about legal fees 'paint a far worse picture'

children.content: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has provided answers to a series of questions about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fees matter, but the Greens say they "paint a far worse picture of this shameful saga and his role in it".

articles.title: What happens next for Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fee saga?

children.content: It's been two weeks since Madeleine Ogilvie resigned from Tasmanian cabinet, and the saga is reaching a tipping point. What could happen when parliament returns?

image.altText: Tasmanian parliament building from outside.

articles.title: TassieCast: Is this really Tasmania's 'most transparent' government?

children.content: Transparency and trust in government are cornerstones of any successful democracy. So how does it feel not knowing why almost half a million dollars of taxpayers money has so far been spent on government MPs' legal bills? Airlie Ward talks with state political reporter Josh Duggan.

gallery.altText: Jeremy Rockliff walks into a room at Government House, surrounded by three other men wearing suits.

thumbnail.altText: Jeremy Rockliff walks into a room at Government House, surrounded by three other men wearing suits.

items.title: Some think Britain's 'broken', and they are looking to Australia for inspiration

children.content: Right-wing populism is rising in the UK where, like Australia, the political landscape is looking very different lately. It has prompted some people to ask if these surging parties can deliver on their promises.

image.altText: A man in a suit smiling and gesturing at the camera, while a group of people behind him hold signs in support of Reform UK.

items.title: Meet the Brisbane couple who have donated more than $100 million to charity

children.content: Quentin and Kylie Birt say they were inspired to donate at the Big Freeze event after reading Neale Daniher's book.

image.altText: A woman and man in hiking gear surrounded by trees.

items.title: Butler open to NDIS changes but convinced it's heading in 'right direction'

children.content: Health Minister Mark Butler is open to adjusting the proposed National Disability Insurance Scheme changes, but insists the government is on the right track.

image.altText: Mark Butler standing in a corridor in a suit

items.title: Nine-year-old Perth girl shot dead by police in Pakistan

children.content: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.

image.altText: A silhouette of two Pakistani police officers holding rifles at a barrier or gate of some sort.

items.title: Love story: When these childhood friends reunited, the spark was undeniable

children.content: It was a case of puppy love when Cambell first set eyes on Tiana at a Gold Coast housing complex when they were both nine years old. They lost touch when they entered their teens but reunited as adults and have been inseparable since.

gallery.altText: Tiana Storm and Cambell Priest smile for the camera in a posed portrait photograph.

thumbnail.altText: Tiana Storm and Cambell Priest smile for the camera in a posed portrait photograph.

items.title: African scam victims left homeless in Cambodia amid crackdown

children.content: Hundreds of trafficking survivors from Africa are left stranded in Cambodia amid a widespread crackdown on the country's scam compounds.

image.altText: Two sets of handcuffs.

items.title: Thousands rally in Belfast to condemn anti-immigrant rioting

children.content: The anti-racism rally came after nights of fiery violence in parts of Northern Ireland following the arrest of a 30-year-old man from Sudan on charges of attempted murder in a stabbing that left a man partly blind.

image.altText: A large crowd holds up anti-racism signs in the middle of a city.

items.title: The feminist and the bra fitter: Inside the world of Hannah Ferguson and her mum

children.content: Emerging media star Hannah Ferguson and her mum, professional bra fitter Phillipa Mitchell, have found themselves on a similar path of empowering women, but just don't expect them to see it that way.

gallery.altText: An older mother and her late twenties daughter look deeply at each other, chatting over cups of tea.

thumbnail.altText: An older mother and her late twenties daughter look deeply at each other, chatting over cups of tea.

items.title: Tinariwen deliver songs of hope from a homeland in crisis

children.content: Legendary African band Tinariwen say there’s very little hope for their country. It just makes their dedication to music more important.

image.altText: Tinariwen stand beside a Toyota pickup truck in a rocky desert landscape with a mountain in the background

items.title: Can you soar to the top of this week's spelling quiz?

children.content: From homophones to Aussie bird names, Guy Montgomery's Spelling Bee quiz is set to test and teach in equal measure.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: Hundreds of regional firefighters rally against levy in Bendigo

children.content: Hundreds of regional Victorian firefighters turn out for a rally in Bendigo, calling on the state government to scrap a controversial levy after saying they have been "left behind".

image.altText: A group of people wearing yellow CFA jackets.

items.title: The multi-million-dollar tourist hotspot where locals can't afford to live

children.content: A tourist hotspot with skyrocketing rents, low rental vacancies and a dearth of public housing, has found a way to help find homes for those who can't afford to live in their own region.

children.content: Once drained for farm irrigation, this former potato farm now attracts birds from as far away as Siberia.

items.title: Woman ditches custom wedding gown for $90 op shop dream dress

items.title: The one of a kind remote NT camp where 'it's cool to go to school'

children.content: Children from across Central Australia have joined a remote Northern Territory camp inside the Watarrka National Park where "it's cool to go to school" — and the footy doesn't hurt either.

items.title: Soccer tournament unites refugees through universal language of sport

children.content: A soccer tournament in Perth's north helps participants overcome barriers, while inspiring more women to pursue the game, as part of Refugee Week.

image.altText: A woman wears a red jumper and holds a soccer ball out to the camera.

items.title: 'Straight to landfill': Compostable packaging but nowhere to compost it

children.content: The ACT mandates reusable, recyclable and compostable packaging at all public events, but nearly all of it ends up in landfill because there is no facility to compost it.

items.title: Police charge Tennant Creek teenager with 21-year-old's stabbing murder
